task_completion.description: Verify that the co-crystal structure PDB:1DVF is loaded into the molecular viewer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for '1DVF' or '1dvf' listed in the object menu/panel of a molecular viewer (like PyMOL), with the 3D macromolecular structure visible in the main viewing area.

task_completion.description: Verify that inter-chain contacts at the antibody-antigen interface are identified.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for visual indicators of molecular interactions between different chains (antibody and antigen), such as dashed lines representing polar contacts/hydrogen bonds, or specific interface residues highlighted/selected in the viewer.

task_completion.description: Verify that the epitope on the lysozyme antigen is characterized.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for specific lysozyme residues labeled or selected in the 3D viewer, or a text editor open showing a draft report documenting the specific residues that make up the epitope.

task_completion.description: Verify that the structure is styled to produce a publication-quality figure highlighting the interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 3D structure being visually styled (e.g., chains colored distinctly, use of surface or high-quality cartoon representations, zoomed in specifically on the interface, or background color adjusted for clarity).

task_completion.description: Verify that the figure is saved to the specified path: ~/PyMOL_Data/images/dvf_interface.png
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a command like 'png ~/PyMOL_Data/images/dvf_interface.png' (or similar save command) in the PyMOL console, a save dialog box, or the file visible in a file manager/terminal at that exact path.

task_completion.description: Verify that the documentation report is saved to the specified path: ~/PyMOL_Data/dvf_interface_report.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor saving the document to '~/PyMOL_Data/dvf_interface_report.txt', or a terminal command confirming the creation/saving of this specific text file.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually used a molecular visualization tool (like PyMOL) to analyze the structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the PyMOL GUI or command-line interface being actively used to load, manipulate, and analyze the 3D structure, rather than just writing a script blindly.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent performed actual analysis to find contacts and the epitope.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of selection commands, distance measurements, or 'find polar contacts' tools in the viewer, rather than hallucinating or pasting a pre-written list of residues.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actively wrote or compiled the report based on its findings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the agent typing or pasting its structural findings into a text editor or using echo/cat commands in the terminal to construct the report file.
